paragraph.title: GenAI hallucinations
paragraph.text: Generative AI systems may produce outputs that are factually incorrect or fabricated, commonly referred to as hallucinations. Where such outputs inform financial decisions or customer communications, financial service providers should establish controls to detect and manage the risk of erroneous or misleading outputs.

source.title: BCBS 239 — Principles for effective risk data aggregation

connections.rationale: BCBS 239's accuracy-and-integrity principle supports 3.11's call for controls over erroneous outputs.

quote.text: A bank should be able to generate accurate and reliable risk data to meet normal and stress/crisis reporting accuracy requirements. Data should be aggregated on a largely automated basis so as to minimise the probability of errors.

connections.rationale: The EU AI Act requires generative outputs to be marked as AI-generated — a transparency obligation 3.11 does not impose.

quote.text: Providers of AI systems generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.

connections.rationale: The Association of Banks agrees that human review of generative outputs should be proportionate to their impact — reinforcing 3.11.

quote.text: Members support a risk-proportionate approach to human review of generative AI outputs, focused on customer-facing and decision-critical use cases.
